response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced generalist with genuine developer-tooling interests, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. The position demands strong Java and Kotlin expertise, deep JVM understanding, and solid Spring ecosystem experience; his Java exposure is minimal and dated, and Kotlin, Spring, and JVM internals are absent from the resume. His only IDE work (NI LabWindows/CVI) was a seven-month C/C++ engagement over a decade ago, while his recent career centers on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms and DLang systems work.
strengths: Prior hands-on IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) plus authored language tooling (D test runner, assertion library)
Broad polyglot engineering background spanning web, desktop, embedded, and high-throughput low-latency systems
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, testing workshops, and sustained open-source maintenance
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, and only brief, dated Java work — the role's core required stack
No evidence of deep JVM understanding or enterprise framework tooling; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js product engineering
IDE-specific experience is short (about seven months) and from 2014-2015, with an unexplained employment gap in 2020
